title: Race-specific strategy prior table from official guides
summary: Official strategy guides repeatedly publish race-level priors such as pit-lane loss, one-stop vs two-stop preference, and compound warm-up caveats.
rationale: Official strategy guides repeatedly publish race-level priors such as pit-lane loss, one-stop vs two-stop preference, and compound warm-up caveats. Those constants vary materially by circuit and can shift stop incentives even when per-row tyre features look similar.
implementation_hint: Build a small external lookup by Race for pit-lane loss bucket, baseline one-stop/two-stop bias, and warm-up sensitivity, sourced from official strategy guides or stable race metadata. Join these as low-cardinality priors and interact them with LapsRemaining_Est, TyreLife_To_Remaining, and compound. Keep the table coarse to avoid brittle manual numbers.
expected_effect: Adds missing track-specific economics of stopping, which should help separate 'must extend' versus 'pit now' races that otherwise share similar on-tyre states.
risk: Manual external priors can be noisy across years and weather; use broad buckets rather than exact seconds, and ensure no post-race information leaks into the lookup.
